article_title: Bylaw 14789 — Housing Agreement (5980 Kathleen Avenue) Bylaw 2025

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby approves housing agreement for 94 non-market rental units at 5980 Kathleen Avenue
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has passed Bylaw 14789, which authorizes a housing agreement with Bosa Properties for 94 non-market rental housing units (rental units with below-market rents) at 5980 Kathleen Avenue. The agreement replaces an earlier bylaw and sets rules for who can rent these units, how rent is set, and how tenants displaced by redevelopment get first chance to return. The units include 92 replacement rental units (for people who had to move because of redevelopment) and 2 required inclusionary units (for households with low-to-moderate incomes).
minutes_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Bylaw No. 14789 
Page 1 of 33 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
CITY OF BURNABY 
 
BYLAW NO. 14789 
 
 
A BYLAW to authorize the execution of a Housing Agreement 
for the non-market rental housing development at 
5980 Kathleen Avenue and repeal Bylaw No. 14726 
 
The Council of the City of Burnaby ENACTS as follows: 
1. 
This Bylaw may be cited as BURNABY HOUSING AGREEMENT (5980 
KATHLEEN AVENUE) BYLAW 2026. 
2. 
The City is hereby authorized to enter into a housing agreement with Bosa 
Properties (Metrotown) Inc., substantially in the form set out in Schedule “A” (the “Housing 
Agreement”), for the non-market rental housing development on lands legally described as: 
PID: 032-473-303, Air Space Parcel 1 District Lot 153 Group 1 New Westminster 
District Air Space Plan EPP144132 
3. 
The Corporate Officer is hereby authorized and empowered to execute the 
Housing Agreement on behalf of the City. 
4. 
Bylaw No. 14726, being the former BURNABY HOUSING AGREEMENT 
(5980 KATHLEEN AVENUE) BYLAW 2026, is hereby repealed. 
FIRST READING this 13th day of January, 2026 
 
SECOND READING this 13th day of January, 2026 
 
THIRD READING this 13th day of January, 2026 
 
RECONSIDERED AND ADOPTED this 27th day of January, 2026 
 
 
 
 
 
                                                     MAYOR 
 
 
 
Signed Original Bylaw on file. 
DEPUTY CORPORATE OFFICER 
 
 

Page 2 of 33 
 
 
 
Housing Agreement (Bosa Properties (Metrotown) Inc. – 5980 Kathleen Ave 
Rezoning Reference #19-42 Subdivision Reference #20-15) 
Market Rental, Replacement and Required Inclusionary Units (with Transferred Replacement Units) 
Schedule “A” 
HOUSING AGREEMENT 
(Section 483 Local Government Act) 
THIS AGREEMENT is dated for reference ______________, 2026, 
 
BETWEEN: 
 
BOSA PROPERTIES (METROTOWN) INC. 
 
Incorporation No. BC0703876 
 
1101 – 838 West Hastings Street 
 
Vancouver, BC  V6C 0A6 
 
(the “Owner”) 
 
AND: 
CITY OF BURNABY 
4949 Canada Way 
Burnaby, B.C.  V5G 1M2 
(the “City”) 
 
WHEREAS: 
A. 
Section 483 of the Local Government Act permits the City to enter into and, by legal 
notation on title, note on title to lands, housing agreements which may include, without 
limitation, conditions with respect to the form of tenure of housing units; 
B. 
The Owner is the registered owner of the Lands and has constructed the Development on 
the Lands and obtained occupancy;  
C. 
The City has established tenant assistance and replacement housing programs for persons 
displaced by redevelopment and, pursuant to Council direction respecting the non-market 
portion of the redevelopment known as “Solhouse” at 6035 Wilson Avenue, certain non-
market housing obligations have been transferred to the Lands, to be fulfilled in accordance 
with the City’s Tenant Assistance Policy; 
D. 
The Owner has agreed with the City to provide, operate and maintain on the Lands a total 
of ninety-four (94) Non-Market Units, of which ninety-two (92) are Replacement Rental 
Units and two (2) are Required Inclusionary Units, all on the terms and conditions of this 
Agreement; 

Page 3 of 33 
 
 
 
Housing Agreement (Bosa Properties (Metrotown) Inc. – 5980 Kathleen Ave 
Rezoning Reference #19-42 Subdivision Reference #20-15) 
Market Rental, Replacement and Required Inclusionary Units (with Transferred Replacement Units) 
E. 
The Parent Parcel was subdivided by the Air Space Plan to create one air space parcel, 
being the Lands containing all of the Non-Market Units, as well as the Remainder Parcel 
containing all of the Market Rental Units; and 
F. 
The Owner and the City wish to enter into this Agreement to provide the Non-Market Units 
as affordable housing to implement the transfer referenced in Recital C and to clarify 
related offer, timing and allocation procedures under the Tenant Assistance Policy on the 
terms and conditions set out in this Agreement.  
NOW THEREFORE in consideration of ten dollars ($10.00) and other good and valuable 
consideration (the receipt and sufficiency of which are acknowledged by both parties), and in 
consideration of the promises exchanged below, the Owner and the City covenant and agree as 
follows: 
ARTICLE 1 
DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 
1.1 
In this Agreement the following words have the following meanings: 
(a) 
“Accessibility Needs” refer to physical accessibility needs identified by a 
Returning Tenant, in their Tenant Assistance Form or subsequently, and 
accommodated, as practicable, in an Adaptable Housing Unit; 
(b) 
“Adaptable Housing Unit” means a Non-Market Unit that: (a) is designed and 
built with features that permit easy modification to accommodate changing 
accessibility requirements over time; and (b) conforms to the requirements and 
standards specified in the Zoning Bylaw and elsewhere in the British Columbia 
Building Code for adaptable dwelling units; 
(c) 
“Agreement” means this agreement together with all schedules, attachments and 
priority agreements attached hereto; 
(d) 
“Air Space Plan” means an air space subdivision plan of the Lands, or any portion 
thereof, pursuant to the Land Title Act;  
(e) 
“BC Housing” means the British Columbia Housing Management Commission;  
(f) 
“Business Day” means a day which is not a Saturday, Sunday or statutory holiday 
(as defined in the Employment Standards Act (British Columbia)) in British 
Columbia;  
(g) 
“Building” refers to the residential apartment building constructed on the Lands 
and the Remainder Parcel, which building contains both the Market Rental Units 
and the Non-Market Units; 

Page 4 of 33 
 
 
 
Housing Agreement (Bosa Properties (Metrotown) Inc. – 5980 Kathleen Ave 
Rezoning Reference #19-42 Subdivision Reference #20-15) 
Market Rental, Replacement and Required Inclusionary Units (with Transferred Replacement Units) 
(h) 
“CD Plan” means the comprehensive development plan for the Parent Parcel 
entitled “Bosa Kathleen” prepared by IBI Group Architects (Canada) Inc. and filed 
with the City’s General Manager Planning and Development; 
(i) 
“City” and “City of Burnaby” means the City of Burnaby and is called the “City” 
when referring to the corporate entity and “City of Burnaby” when referring to the 
geographic location; 
(j) 
“CMHC” means the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation; 
(k) 
“CMHC Market Median Rent” means the median residential apartment rent 
applicable to areas within the City of Burnaby (being Central Park / Metrotown 
CMHC Market Rental survey zone in the case of the Non-Market Units under this 
Agreement), based on rental market data collected by CMHC, and if such rental 
market data is no longer published by CMHC, then such other methodology 
established by the City at its discretion; 
(l) 
“Daily Amount” means $100.00 per day as of January 1, 2018, and adjusted 
annually on January 1st of each subsequent year by a percentage equal to the 
percentage of the increase in the Vancouver Headline CPI for the period from 
January 1 to December 31 of the preceding calendar year;  
(m) 
“Development” means the development of a 35-storey purpose-built rental 
residential apartment building with underground parking constructed on the Parent 
Parcel in accordance with the CD Plan; 
(n) 
“Effective Date” has the meaning ascribed to it in Section 11.2; 
(o) 
“Eligibility Date” means the date a tenancy is considered eligible for benefits under 
the Tenant Assistance Policy; 
(p) 
“Eligible Tenant” means: 
(i) 
in the case of a Replacement Rental Unit, a Household with at least one 
member who is a Returning Tenant; and 
(ii) 
in the case of a Required Inclusionary Unit, a Household with a gross 
household income that does not exceed the Housing Income Limits (HILs) 
for the applicable unit type (or such other income threshold approved by the 
City at its discretion), but excluding: (A) the Owner, any directors, officers, 
and employees of the Owner, as applicable, and their respective direct 
family members; and (B) if the Owner contracts a third party to manage and 
administer the Non-Market U
